title: Gordonia integerrima
taxonomy: Gordonia integerrima is classified in the family Fabaceae, but it is more commonly associated with the family Gordoniaceae, which is a small and relatively recent classification. However, some sources group Gordonia integerrima in the family Celastraceae. This classification may be due to the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary history. More research is needed to confirm the plant's classification with certainty. The classification of Gordonia integerrima is not universally agreed upon among botanists.
uses: Gordonia integerrima, a species native to Madagascar, has been found to have various medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
